sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Methoxyflurane delivered via a Penthrox inhaler provides short-term inhaled analgesia under clinician supervision. It is most useful when the patient can self-administer and when fast, titratable pain relief is needed during assessment, splinting, extrication, or transport.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.content:
1. Prepare the inhaler according to the device instructions and ensure the activated charcoal chamber is fitted

2. Load the methoxyflurane dose as supplied and confirm the device is ready for use

3. Explain to the patient that they will self-administer by inhaling through the mouthpiece under supervision

4. Encourage normal inhalation and reassess pain, sedation, and tolerance frequently

5. Continue only while the patient remains able to self-administer safely and the analgesic benefit outweighs side effects

sections.title: Key Points
sections.content:
- Patient self-administration is part of the safety model; loss of ability to hold or use the device should prompt stopping it and reassessment
- Ensure good cabin or room ventilation to reduce clinician exposure to exhaled vapour
- Review contraindications and cautions carefully before use, especially significant renal disease, methoxyflurane hypersensitivity, or malignant hyperthermia risk
- Penthrox should not distract from reassessment, splinting, or escalation to other analgesic strategies when needed
